gaps.domain: AI Agent Browser Automation & Computer Use
gaps.title: Human-in-the-Loop Orchestration for Browser Agents
gaps.what_to_build: A middleware SDK and dashboard that wraps any browser agent (Browser Use, Stagehand, Skyvern) with: automatic failure/uncertainty detection, human takeover UI showing live browser state, approval workflows for sensitive actions (purchases, PII forms), manual correction capture that feeds back into agent learning, and escalation policies (timeout, confidence threshold, action type).
gaps.who_needs_it: Engineering teams deploying browser agents in production where failures have real consequences: e-commerce ops teams, financial services automating compliance workflows, HR teams automating recruiting pipelines, and any team where agent errors cost money or trust.

gaps.title: Developer-Friendly Model Conversion & Optimization Hub
gaps.what_to_build: A unified web service and CLI that takes any HuggingFace model and produces optimized, benchmarked artifacts for all major edge runtimes (GGUF, ONNX, Core ML, ExecuTorch .pte, TensorRT, QNN, LiteRT). Auto-selects optimal quantization per target device. Includes a hosted artifact registry with per-device performance metadata.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers and mobile developers deploying models across diverse edge targets. Anyone who has wrestled with 7+ incompatible model formats and broken conversion pipelines.

gaps.domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs
gaps.title: Unified HuggingFace-Compatible Function Calling Data Format & Converter
gaps.what_to_build: A Python library + HuggingFace dataset standard for function calling data. Includes: (1) a canonical schema (tools, messages, calls, results), (2) converters from/to xLAM, Glaive, ToolBench, ToolACE, OpenAI, Anthropic formats, (3) HuggingFace dataset card template, (4) validation and statistics CLI. Publish as 'fc-datasets' on PyPI.
gaps.who_needs_it: Every ML engineer training function calling models â€” currently ~5K-10K active practitioners. Also dataset creators who want their data adopted widely.

gaps.title: Reward Function Marketplace / Reusable Reward Library
gaps.what_to_build: An open-source registry of tested, documented reward functions organized by domain (math, code, science, legal, medical) with a standard API. Users browse, fork, compose, and share reward functions. Includes reward function testing harness, compatibility layer across TRL/OpenRLHF/veRL, and community ratings. Think 'npm for reward functions'.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers starting GRPO training who spend days writing reward functions from scratch. Domain experts who know what 'correct' looks like but can't express it as code. Teams migrating between frameworks.

gaps.title: Quantization-Aware Reasoning Evaluation Suite
gaps.what_to_build: An open-source evaluation suite that systematically tests reasoning quality across quantization levels (FP16, INT8, INT4, INT2) on diverse reasoning tasks. Includes multi-step chain degradation analysis, error propagation tracking across reasoning steps, and calibration metrics. Outputs a quantization-reasoning degradation report showing exactly where and how quantization breaks reasoning chains.
gaps.who_needs_it: Model compression engineers, edge deployment teams, quantization researchers, and r/LocalLLaMA community members who routinely quantize models but have no standardized way to measure reasoning impact. Red Hat ran 500K+ quantized evals and found significant reasoning-specific degradation â€” practitioners need this daily.

gaps.domain: AI Agent Browser Automation & Computer Use
gaps.title: Browser Agent Security & Sandboxing Layer
gaps.what_to_build: A security middleware that wraps any browser agent with: domain allowlists/blocklists, action type restrictions (no purchases without approval), PII detection/redaction in agent context, credential vault integration (1Password, HashiCorp Vault), prompt injection detection, comprehensive audit trails, and configurable security policies.
gaps.who_needs_it: Any enterprise deploying browser agents. Security teams evaluating agent adoption. Compliance officers in regulated industries. Development teams building agent-powered products for end users.

gaps.title: KG-SLM Integration for Agentic Workflows
gaps.what_to_build: An open-source SLM-native agent memory library that stores and retrieves structured knowledge via a lightweight KG, with adapters for LangGraph, CrewAI, and AutoGen. Optimized for models under 7B with compact graph serialization and efficient subgraph retrieval for agent context windows.
gaps.who_needs_it: AI engineers building cost-efficient autonomous agents, startups that cannot afford cloud LLM API costs for multi-step agent workflows, edge robotics teams needing persistent memory.

gaps.title: No SLM-optimized reward model training toolkit
gaps.what_to_build: A lightweight Python library for training PRMs, ORMs, and verifiers specifically for 1B-7B models on a single consumer GPU (24GB). Built on top of PEFT/QLoRA with optimized memory management. Includes pre-configured recipes for common SLMs (Qwen, Phi, Llama-small, Gemma). One-command training: `verifier train --base qwen-3b --data my_prefs.jsonl --type prm`.
gaps.who_needs_it: Independent ML researchers, hobbyists on r/LocalLLaMA, startups with limited GPU budgets, university labs. The massive community that trains SLMs on consumer hardware but currently can only use rule-based GRPO rewards because RM training is too resource-intensive.

gaps.domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs
gaps.title: End-to-End SLM Function Calling Toolkit (Data Gen â†’ Train â†’ Eval â†’ Deploy)
gaps.what_to_build: Open-source CLI/SDK that unifies: (1) synthetic data generation from OpenAPI/Swagger specs using open-source LLMs, (2) LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning via Unsloth/TRL, (3) BFCL-compatible evaluation, (4) one-click export to GGUF/ONNX for edge deployment. Think 'create-react-app' for function calling SLMs.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers building on-device AI agents, startups shipping edge AI products, enterprise teams deploying internal tool-use agents. ~50K+ active practitioners based on HuggingFace model downloads for function calling models.

gaps.title: Tool-Use & Agentic Reasoning Data Generator
gaps.what_to_build: A platform that generates multi-step tool-calling and API-usage reasoning traces at scale. Users define available tools (APIs, databases, code interpreters), and the system generates diverse scenarios where an agent must reason through which tools to call, in what order, with what parameters, and how to interpret results. Outputs formatted for SFT and RLHF/RLVR training.
gaps.who_needs_it: Agentic AI developers, companies building AI assistants that use tools (Cursor, Devin-like products), API integration platforms, enterprise teams building internal AI agents. Gartner says 40% of enterprise apps will embed task-specific agents by 2026.

gaps.title: On-Device RAG Pipeline Framework
gaps.what_to_build: An open-source, on-device RAG SDK that bundles local vector storage, document chunking, embedding model, and retrieval pipeline -- all running on-device with zero cloud dependency. Supports iOS, Android, and desktop. Ships with pre-optimized embedding models for mobile.
gaps.who_needs_it: Enterprise mobile app developers needing private document Q&A (legal, healthcare, finance). Field workers who need offline knowledge retrieval. Any app adding 'chat with your documents' features locally.

gaps.title: Custom domain verifier training is extremely manual
gaps.what_to_build: A platform with domain verifier templates, guided workflows, and automated reward function generation for domains beyond math/code. Users pick a domain (legal, medical, finance, science), provide example problems with solutions, and the platform generates verifiable reward functions using rubric decomposition (inspired by RaR/K2V approaches). Includes a template library, synthetic data generator for the domain, and evaluation harness.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers at enterprises applying LLM reasoning to domain-specific tasks (legal analysis, medical diagnosis, financial modeling). Research labs exploring RLVR in new domains. Startups building vertical AI products that need verified reasoning.

gaps.title: End-to-End Reasoning Distillation Pipeline (No-Code/Low-Code)
gaps.what_to_build: A unified platform with visual pipeline builder that covers the full distillation workflow: teacher model selection, reasoning trace generation (via API or local inference), automated trace filtering/curation, student model training (SFT + optional RL), evaluation with reasoning-specific metrics, and one-click deployment. Integrate vLLM/Ollama for generation, TRL/Unsloth for training, and lm-eval for evaluation behind a single UI.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers at startups (500K+ globally), applied AI teams at mid-size companies, researchers without deep infra expertise, and enterprises wanting to reduce frontier model API costs by distilling into owned models.

gaps.title: Plug-and-Play Reasoning Enhancement Layer for Existing SLMs
gaps.what_to_build: A model-agnostic Python SDK that wraps ANY SLM (Phi, Qwen, Gemma, Llama-small) with reasoning search strategies (MCTS, beam search, self-consistency, best-of-N) via a simple API: `reasoner = ReasonEnhance(model); result = reasoner.solve(prompt, strategy='mcts', budget=10)`. Includes pre-built PRM adapters, automatic strategy selection based on query type, and OpenAI-compatible API endpoints. Integrates with vLLM/SGLang backends for serving.
gaps.who_needs_it: Application developers (millions globally) who use SLMs but lack ML expertise to implement search algorithms. Startups building AI products on open-source SLMs. Enterprise teams wanting better reasoning without switching models. Estimated 500K+ developers deploying SLMs in production by mid-2026.

gaps.title: Difficulty-Calibrated Reasoning Curriculum Generator
gaps.what_to_build: A system that assesses a model's current reasoning capabilities, then generates progressively harder training examples that match the model's learning frontier. Uses adaptive difficulty calibration (not static difficulty levels) â€” continuously re-evaluates as the model improves. Integrates with RL training loops.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers fine-tuning reasoning models, research labs doing RLVR/RLHF, education technology companies building adaptive learning systems, teams wanting to minimize compute waste on too-easy or too-hard examples.

gaps.title: Context Quality Scoring and Observability Dashboard
Visual Context Window Debugger for Agent Development
Quantized KG-Augmented SLM Inference Engine
gaps.what_to_build: A unified inference engine that natively loads quantized SLMs (GGUF/AWQ) and co-locates a compact graph index, optimizing the retrieval-generation loop end-to-end. Single binary deployment with built-in KG indexing from documents.
gaps.who_needs_it: Local AI enthusiasts (r/LocalLLaMA), privacy-conscious enterprises, developers in regions with expensive cloud compute, air-gapped deployments.

gaps.title: Cross-Platform Mobile LLM SDK (React Native / Flutter / KMP)
gaps.what_to_build: An open-source, cross-platform mobile LLM SDK with native bindings for React Native, Flutter, and Kotlin Multiplatform. Wraps llama.cpp/ExecuTorch with idiomatic APIs for each framework. Includes streaming, conversation management, and model download/caching.
gaps.who_needs_it: Cross-platform mobile developers (60%+ of mobile dev workforce uses React Native or Flutter). Teams that want on-device LLM features without writing native C++/Swift/Kotlin.

gaps.title: Agent Circuit Breaker Library
gaps.what_to_build: A Python library that implements the circuit breaker pattern specifically for AI agent tool calls. Unlike generic circuit breakers (pybreaker), this understands agent semantics: tracks per-tool failure rates, automatically disables failing tools and notifies the agent to use alternatives, provides graceful degradation to text-only mode, and integrates with agent frameworks' tool registries.
gaps.who_needs_it: Teams running agents with multiple external tool integrations (APIs, databases, search). Production agents that currently keep hammering failing tools until timeout, wasting API credits and causing cascading slowdowns.

gaps.title: Cost-Optimal Distillation Budget Calculator
gaps.what_to_build: A tool/library that predicts: optimal number of reasoning traces for target quality, best teacher model for quality/cost ratio, training compute budget estimation, diminishing returns detection during trace generation, and comparison of distillation strategies (few-shot curated vs. large-scale generation). Includes a cost simulator UI and integrates with popular cloud pricing APIs.
gaps.who_needs_it: Budget-conscious startup ML teams, researchers planning distillation experiments, enterprises evaluating build vs. buy for model distillation, and anyone deciding between Sky-T1-style (5K curated) vs. brute-force (100K+) approaches.

gaps.who_needs_it: Any team running browser agents at scale. Startups with limited budgets. Enterprises optimizing per-automation unit economics. Infrastructure teams managing agent costs.

gaps.what_to_build: A production pipeline that takes a domain, sample problems, and a policy model, then automatically generates step-labeled PRM training data. Users configure strategy, run pipeline, get labeled dataset.
gaps.who_needs_it: Anyone training PRMs. The annotation cost is the #1 bottleneck -- PRM800K required human experts and is still the gold standard years later because generating equivalent quality data is so hard. Research labs, companies building reasoning models, teams adapting PRMs to new domains.

gaps.domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs

Components: (1) 50K+ reasoning-enhanced function calling examples, (2) training recipe using Unsloth/TRL with reasoning tokens, (3) evaluation harness measuring both reasoning quality and call accuracy. Model thinks step-by-step before deciding which tool to call.
gaps.who_needs_it: Developers building complex multi-step agent workflows on edge devices. Teams where single-turn accuracy isn't enough â€” need planning over tool sequences.

gaps.what_to_build: An error classification middleware that intercepts agent errors, classifies them (transient/permanent, model/tool/infra, recoverable/fatal), and routes each to the optimal recovery strategy. Routes: transient errors to retry with backoff, model errors to prompt adjustment, tool errors to fallback tools, permanent errors to human escalation.
gaps.who_needs_it: Any team running agents in production that wastes compute on retrying permanent errors or escalates transient errors unnecessarily. Platform teams building agent infrastructure who need to reduce MTTR and cost-per-error.

gaps.title: Domain-Specific Reasoning Data Generation Platform
gaps.what_to_build: A platform for generating reasoning training data in regulated/vertical domains: legal (contract analysis, case reasoning), medical (diagnostic reasoning, treatment planning), finance (risk assessment, compliance reasoning), accounting (audit trails). Includes domain-specific ontologies, compliance-aware generation, and expert-reviewed templates.
gaps.who_needs_it: Enterprise AI teams in financial services, healthcare providers building clinical AI, legal tech companies, accounting/audit firms. GPT-4 gets only 16.58% on accounting reasoning â€” massive improvement opportunity.

gaps.domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs

gaps.title: Multi-Agent Memory Governance & Consistency Layer
gaps.what_to_build: A memory middleware layer that sits between multi-agent orchestrators (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph) and storage backends, providing configurable consistency models (eventual/causal/strong), conflict resolution policies, and memory access control. SDK for Python/JS with adapters for popular frameworks.
gaps.who_needs_it: Enterprise AI platform teams building multi-agent systems, CrewAI/AutoGen/LangGraph power users, companies running concurrent agent workflows on shared data.

gaps.title: Unified Agent Resilience Middleware
gaps.what_to_build: A composable Python middleware layer that wraps any agent framework (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, Pydantic AI, custom) and provides retry logic, circuit breakers, fallback providers, output validation, and crash recovery as pluggable decorators/middleware. Think 'Tenacity + pybreaker + Guardrails AI + checkpointing' in one package with a @resilient decorator.
gaps.who_needs_it: Backend engineers and ML engineers running production AI agents across any framework. Companies with 2-50 agents in production that spend weeks stitching together resilience patterns manually. DevOps teams responsible for agent uptime SLAs.

gaps.title: Open Source Alternative to OpenAI Distillation API
gaps.what_to_build: An open-source platform that replicates OpenAI's stored completions + evals + fine-tuning workflow but for any open model (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral). gaps.title: Adaptive Compute Budget Controller for SLM Reasoning
gaps.what_to_build: A middleware service that dynamically allocates test-time compute based on query difficulty and latency SLOs. Features: difficulty classifier trained on SLM confidence signals, configurable compute budget tiers (1x-100x), real-time latency SLO enforcement, cost tracking dashboard, and integration with vLLM/SGLang serving stacks. Implements budget forcing (s1-style), Dynasor-style dynamic allocation, and conformal risk control for compute budgets.
gaps.who_needs_it: Production ML teams serving reasoning SLMs at scale (estimated 50K+ teams globally). Cloud AI platform teams (AWS, Azure, GCP). Cost-conscious startups where reasoning model inference is their largest expense. Any team paying for GPU compute on reasoning workloads.

gaps.title: Automated Reasoning Trace Verification & Quality Assurance Platform
gaps.what_to_build: An automated pipeline that verifies the correctness of generated reasoning traces using formal methods (math proof checkers), code execution, logic validators, and LLM-as-judge ensembles. Includes contamination detection, consistency checking, and quality scoring dashboards. Plugs into any generation pipeline.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers building reasoning models who currently 'eyeball' quality, AI safety researchers needing verified training data, benchmark creators guarding against contamination, enterprise teams needing auditable training data.

gaps.title: Hybrid Edge-Cloud Inference Orchestrator
gaps.what_to_build: An open-source SDK that intelligently routes LLM queries between on-device SLMs and cloud LLMs based on query complexity, latency budget, cost constraints, and connectivity status. Includes a confidence estimator, fallback logic, and a unified API that abstracts the routing decision.
gaps.who_needs_it: Enterprise developers needing reliability guarantees. Any app where on-device SLMs handle 80% of queries but need cloud escalation for complex ones. Telecom, fintech, healthcare apps.

gaps.title: Memory Observability & Debugging Platform
gaps.what_to_build: Memory-specific observability layer: instrument memory reads/writes across Mem0/Zep/Letta/custom stores, provide memory timeline visualization, retrieval quality scoring (precision/recall), memory diff between time periods, alerting on anomalous memory mutations. OpenTelemetry-compatible.
gaps.who_needs_it: AI platform teams debugging agent behavior, ML engineers optimizing memory retrieval, security teams monitoring for memory poisoning, product teams understanding why agents make certain decisions.

gaps.title: Framework-Agnostic Error Recovery SDK
gaps.what_to_build: A Python SDK with @recoverable, @checkpoint, @validate decorators that work across LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, Pydantic AI, Google ADK, and custom agent loops. Provides: retry with classification, output validation, state checkpointing, and human escalation -- all via simple decorators that wrap any function. Framework adapters handle the translation layer.
gaps.who_needs_it: Teams using multiple agent frameworks or migrating between them. Startups and mid-size companies that don't want vendor lock-in. Developers building custom agents who want production-grade recovery without adopting a full framework.

gaps.title: Curriculum / Difficulty Scheduling for RL Training
gaps.what_to_build: A library that plugs into TRL/OpenRLHF/veRL to automate curriculum scheduling during GRPO training. Auto-estimates example difficulty (via reward variance, model confidence, or pre-scoring), then schedules easy-to-hard progression. Implements DUMP (UCB-based distribution sampling), VCRL (variance-based), and E2H approaches from recent papers.
gaps.who_needs_it: Resource-constrained teams (startups, academics) who need sample-efficient training. Anyone training on mixed-difficulty datasets where flat sampling wastes compute on too-easy or too-hard examples.

gaps.title: RL Training Observability / Debugging Dashboard
gaps.what_to_build: A specialized dashboard for RL training runs that goes beyond W&B/TensorBoard. Tracks GRPO-specific metrics: group reward distributions, advantage estimation quality, entropy collapse detection, homogeneous response alerts, reward hacking indicators, policy drift monitoring. Integrates as a callback into TRL/OpenRLHF.
gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers debugging RL training failures. Teams spending GPU hours on runs that silently degrade. Anyone who's had a training run collapse due to entropy loss or reward hacking without warning.

gaps.title: Cost-Optimized Reasoning Data Generation (Small Model Bootstrapping)
gaps.what_to_build: A cost-aware reasoning data generation platform that routes easy examples to cheap models and hard examples to expensive models. Includes budget management, quality-per-dollar metrics, cost forecasting, and iterative bootstrapping with exponential budget allocation. Supports self-improvement loops where small models progressively generate harder data.
gaps.who_needs_it: Budget-constrained startups, academic researchers, small teams who need reasoning data but cannot afford millions in API costs. 'Generation cost often exceeds training cost' â€” confirmed pain point.

gaps.title: Reasoning Trace Diversity & Coverage Analyzer
gaps.what_to_build: An analytics dashboard that maps reasoning pattern coverage in a dataset: what types of reasoning are present (causal, mathematical, deductive, analogical, etc.), where blind spots exist, what's over/under-represented. Uses entropy metrics, taxonomy mapping, and visual distribution reports.
gaps.who_needs_it: Dataset curators building reasoning training sets, teams doing data mixture optimization, researchers studying reasoning capabilities. 'Data Recipes for Reasoning Models' (2025) confirms need for systematic coverage analysis.

gaps.title: SLM-Specific Reasoning Evaluation Platform with Edge Deployment Metrics
gaps.what_to_build: A unified platform that evaluates SLM reasoning quality jointly with edge deployment constraints: latency budget, memory footprint, thermal throttling, battery drain. Introduces 'reasoning quality per watt' and 'reasoning accuracy at X ms latency budget' as first-class metrics. Integrates with device profilers (Android, iOS, embedded Linux).
gaps.who_needs_it: Edge ML engineers, mobile AI developers, IoT AI product teams. AWS is deploying fine-tuned SLMs at the edge for telco (semantic MCP server). On-device LLM adoption in 2026 is surging, but teams lack eval tooling that bridges reasoning and runtime constraints.

gaps.title: No tree search + verifier inference toolkit for SLMs
gaps.what_to_build: A production-ready inference library: plug in your SLM + verifier, configure search strategy (MCTS, beam search, best-of-N, weighted voting), get verified reasoning outputs via API. Optimized for latency with batched verification, caching, and early termination. Deployable as a server or embedded library.
gaps.who_needs_it: Teams deploying SLMs in production who want to boost accuracy without scaling model size. Companies using SLMs for reasoning tasks (math tutoring, code generation, analytical workflows) where correctness matters more than latency.

gaps.title: Automated Hardware-Aware Model Selection & Optimization
gaps.what_to_build: A library/service that profiles target device hardware (RAM, NPU type, thermal budget, available storage) and automatically selects the optimal model + quantization + runtime configuration. Includes a device capability database covering 1000+ Android SKUs and a recommendation API.
gaps.who_needs_it: Android developers targeting diverse device fleets. Any team deploying LLMs to millions of devices with wildly different hardware capabilities (from budget phones to flagships).

gaps.title: On-Device Structured Output / JSON Mode Guarantees
gaps.what_to_build: A constrained decoding library optimized for mobile/edge runtimes that guarantees valid JSON/structured output from on-device LLMs. Integrates with llama.cpp, ExecuTorch, and ONNX Runtime. Supports JSON Schema, grammar-based constraints, and enum enforcement.
gaps.who_needs_it: Enterprise app developers needing reliable LLM output parsing on-device. Any app where LLM output feeds into structured workflows (form filling, data extraction, API calls).

gaps.title: Domain-Specific Reasoning Distillation Templates (Beyond Math/Code)

Each template includes: domain-specific teacher prompts, curated seed datasets, domain-aware reward models, evaluation benchmarks with domain expert validation, and compliance guardrails (HIPAA for medical, regulatory for financial). Templates plug into any distillation pipeline.
gaps.who_needs_it: Healthcare AI teams (60%+ of financial institutions running AI pilots), legal tech companies (45% of AmLaw 200 firms exploring domain-tuned models), scientific computing teams, and any vertical AI company needing domain reasoning beyond math/code.

gaps.what_to_build: A comprehensive evaluation framework that measures: reasoning faithfulness to teacher (not just final answer accuracy), step-by-step quality via CoTQS metrics, reasoning efficiency (tokens per correct answer), failure mode taxonomy and analysis, calibration of confidence vs. accuracy, and regression detection across model versions. Plugs into existing training loops (TRL, Unsloth) and CI/CD pipelines.
gaps.who_needs_it: Researchers comparing distillation methods, teams validating distilled models before production deployment, MLOps teams running continuous evaluation, and anyone publishing distillation results needing standardized metrics.

gaps.who_needs_it: Developer teams building and maintaining browser agent workflows. DevOps teams monitoring agent reliability. Product teams tracking automation success rates and costs.

gaps.domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs
gaps.title: Cross-Platform On-Device Function Calling (iOS + Android + Web + Desktop)

gaps.who_needs_it: Cross-platform app developers, companies with multi-platform products wanting a single AI agent model.

gaps.title: Memory Version Control & Branching
gaps.what_to_build: Memory version control library: snapshot memory state, create branches for A/B testing, diff memory between points in time, merge branches with conflict resolution, rollback to previous states. Git-like CLI + Python SDK. Integrates with existing memory stores.
gaps.who_needs_it: AI product teams doing A/B testing of agent behavior, developers debugging agent decisions, enterprises needing memory audit trails, safety teams testing agent behavior under different memory states.

gaps.title: Self-Improving SLM Reasoning Loop (Online Learning)
gaps.what_to_build: A production system enabling SLMs to continuously improve reasoning from deployment feedback: (1) online collection of reasoning successes/failures with user signals, (2) periodic PRM update loop using new data, (3) ReasoningBank-style memory for retrieving similar past reasoning, (4) automatic search strategy refinement based on performance metrics, (5) safety guardrails to prevent regression. Implements Google's ReasoningBank (20% improvement on 1.7B model) in production.
gaps.who_needs_it: Teams wanting reasoning models that improve over time without manual retraining. Enterprise AI teams with domain-specific feedback loops. Production systems where user corrections are available.

gaps.title: Free/Open Model Marketplace with Edge-Optimized Artifacts
gaps.what_to_build: A model marketplace with pre-quantized, pre-compiled, benchmarked model artifacts for each runtime+device combination. One-click deployment to ExecuTorch, LiteRT, QNN, Core ML, GGUF. Per-device performance guarantees and community ratings.
gaps.who_needs_it: All edge LLM developers who want to skip the conversion/optimization step and deploy a proven model artifact directly.

gaps.title: One-Click GRPO Training for Non-ML Engineers
gaps.what_to_build: A web-based platform where domain experts upload a dataset of questions + answers, select a base model, and get a GRPO-trained reasoning model back. No Python required. Handles reward function generation from examples, hyperparameter selection, training, and evaluation automatically.
gaps.who_needs_it: Domain experts (lawyers, doctors, analysts) who want custom reasoning models. Product engineers at startups. Non-ML teams who read about GRPO and want to try it.

gaps.title: CI/CD-Native SLM Reasoning Evaluation Pipeline
gaps.what_to_build: A turnkey CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Action + CLI) specifically for SLM reasoning evaluation. Runs a curated subset of reasoning benchmarks in <5 minutes, provides pass/fail gating calibrated for SLM reasoning thresholds, and posts regression reports as PR comments. Leverages tinyBenchmarks (140x speedup) for fast iteration.
gaps.who_needs_it: DevOps/MLOps teams, SLM development teams, CI engineers. Promptfoo, Evidently, and Braintrust offer general LLM CI/CD evals but require significant configuration for SLM reasoning. No pre-built pipeline exists for SLM reasoning gates.

gaps.domain: AI Agent Identity, Authentication & Access Control
gaps.title: Runtime Least-Privilege Enforcement for AI Agents
gaps.what_to_build: Runtime authorization engine that dynamically scopes agent permissions per-task. Mints ephemeral tokens with only the permissions needed for the current action. Integrates with OPA for policy-as-code. Provides a decision API that agent frameworks call before each tool invocation. Includes a policy editor UI and pre-built policies for common patterns.
gaps.who_needs_it: Security-conscious enterprises deploying autonomous agents. Platform teams building agent orchestration. Companies in regulated industries (finance, healthcare) where over-permissioned agents are compliance violations.

gaps.domain: AI Agent Identity, Authentication & Access Control
gaps.title: Agent Compliance & Audit Layer (SOC2/HIPAA/GDPR)
gaps.what_to_build: Compliance layer that maps agent actions to specific SOC2/HIPAA/GDPR controls. Generates audit-ready reports showing which agent performed what action, mapped to which compliance control. Real-time compliance violation alerts. Pre-built control mappings for common agent patterns. Integrates with existing GRC tools (Vanta, Drata).
gaps.who_needs_it: Compliance teams at healthcare, financial services, and SaaS companies deploying agents. Auditors needing agent-specific evidence. Companies going through SOC2 audits with agent deployments.

gaps.title: Cross-Architecture Reasoning Distillation (Transformer -> Non-Transformer)
gaps.what_to_build: A general framework for distilling reasoning capabilities across model architectures: Transformer to Mamba/Mamba-2, RWKV, SSM, or hybrid architectures. Architecture-agnostic distillation loss functions, automatic architecture adaptation layers, and benchmarking across target architectures. Extend Llamba's approach beyond Mamba-specific implementation.
gaps.who_needs_it: Researchers exploring efficient architectures, on-device ML teams wanting non-Transformer inference benefits (linear scaling, lower memory), and hardware companies designing AI accelerators for alternative architectures.

gaps.title: Process Reward Model (PRM) Training and Serving Toolkit
gaps.what_to_build: An end-to-end toolkit for training, evaluating, and serving process reward models. Includes step-level annotation tools, PRM training recipes (including data-efficient ThinkPRM approach), serving infrastructure for use in RL training loops, and integration with TRL/OpenRLHF.
gaps.who_needs_it: Advanced RL practitioners who need better-than-binary rewards. Teams where outcome-based rewards lead to reward hacking. Researchers building reward models.

gaps.title: Reasoning Data Marketplace / Sharing Hub
gaps.what_to_build: A marketplace for buying, selling, and sharing curated reasoning datasets. Quality ratings, domain tags, licensing framework, contamination certificates, preview samples. Sellers upload reasoning datasets; buyers search by domain, reasoning type, difficulty, and format.
gaps.who_needs_it: Small teams without GPU budgets, enterprises wanting pre-built domain reasoning data, data sellers with unique domain expertise. Prime Intellect's SYNTHETIC-1 release (900K samples) shows demand for shared reasoning data.

gaps.title: Open-Source SLM+KG Managed Service
gaps.what_to_build: A managed platform: upload documents, auto-build KG, attach SLM, get API endpoint. Pay-per-query pricing. Open-source core with hosted tier.
gaps.who_needs_it: Startups and indie developers who want KG+SLM without ops burden. Small teams without DevOps capacity.

gaps.domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs

gaps.domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs
